Descripcion_Completa:
This is where your work makes a difference.

At Baxter, we believe every person—regardless of who they are or where they are from—deserves a chance to live a healthy life. It was our founding belief in 1931 and continues to be our guiding principle. We are redefining healthcare delivery to make a greater impact today, tomorrow, and beyond.

Our Baxter colleagues are united by our Mission to Save and Sustain Lives. Together, our community is driven by a culture of courage, trust, and collaboration. Every individual is empowered to take ownership and make a meaningful impact. We strive for efficient and effective operations, and we hold each other accountable for delivering exceptional results.

Here, you will find more than just a job—you will find purpose and pride.

Summary:

We are looking for an Automation Engineer based at our Sabiñanigo plant that will be responsible for ensuring optimal performance, reliability, and stability of automated equipment across multiple manufacturing sites at a regional and global level.

Reporting directly to the Regional Engineering Leader, this engineer will be part of a Regional Team and will focus on PLC/HMI programming, data communication, and automation solving to continuously improve the efficiency and consistency of packaging processes in a GMP environment.

What you will be doing:
Fix and optimize PLC and HMI systems on automated production lines.
Develop and modify control logic to improve process stability and reduce downtime.
Integrate SCADA and MES systems for data collection and performance tracking.
Lead effective root cause analyses and corrective actions for automation-related issues.
Collaborate with mechanical, electrical, and maintenance teams to ensure cohesive system functionality.
Implement successfully software change controls according to GMP and validation procedures.
Support digitalization and Industry 4.0 initiatives across Baxter sites.
Provide training to site technicians and engineers on control systems procedures.
Contribute to continuous improvement projects focused on Overall Equipment Effectiveness, waste reduction, and process reliability.

What you will bring:
Degree or equivalent experience in Automation, Software, Control Engineering or a similar field.
Proven technical exposure to automation of filling/packaging technology in a manufacturing/ production environment, ideally in the pharma or healthcare industry.
Strong knowledge acquired through experience of PLC programming skills and HMI and SCADA development.
Solid understanding of automation architectures and system integration.
Analytical and structured problem-solving approach.
Able to communicate optimally in English in a work environment.
Availability to travel 80% of the time within regional or international contexts.

Why do you want to work at Baxter?

Competitive total compensation package
Professional development opportunities
High importance placed on work life balance (Family Responsible Certificate EFR)
Flexible benefits (childcare vouchers, employee discounts, etc.)
Commitment to growing and developing an inclusive and diverse workforce

Titulo: Energy Laboratory Test Means Testing Engineer
Descripcion_Completa:
Job Description:

Job Description:

Come Join Our Team!

Flight and Integration Test Center is growing. If you have the passion, ambition, energy and competence to make a step change in this transformation - we want you!. Your dream job starts here!.

If your profile matches with our requirements and you are interested in joining our dynamic and multicultural team of professionals, you can complete your application online (providing your CV and cover letter outlining your motivation).

Join Airbus now and embrace the opportunity to make a difference. We make it fly and you can too.

As part of Airbus, the job holder will be accountable for operation and maintenance of energy laboratory test means devoted to electrical / pneumatic / fuel / hydraulic / mechanical aircraft systems.

The job holder has direct accountability to Airbus Head of LTM Energy Lab Development & Operation.

Your Main Responsibilities:

Design electro-mechanical test means, including technologies to be applied, instrumentation, materials, etc, to fulfill requirements.

Design control and acquisition software for electro-mechanical test means, including technologies to be applied, instrumentation, safety alarms, etc, to fulfil requirements.

Design and commissioning test set-up facilities, including calibration of sensors/transducers.

Define test procedures to be executed on the test bench based on Laboratory Test Requirements delivered by Design Offices.

Implement test procedures ensuring proper test execution and consistency of data recorded.

Analyse test data ensuring proper pass/fail criteria of the unit/system under test.

Write laboratory test reports supporting validation and verification activities of the overall A/C system development.

Ensure test quality on external deliveries.

Maintain calibrated and inventoried material laboratory database.

Maintain industrial facilities test means ensuring laboratory availability.

Identify and implement proper corrective actions in case of malfunction of industrial test means.

Attend test campaign meetings (Kick-Off Meeting, follow-up meeting and troubleshooting for problem investigation and problem resolution).

Identify technical risks and opportunities for on-going activities and ensure the execution of adequate mitigation plans.

Establish a strong link and customer oriented culture with Design Offices interfaces.

Who We Are Looking For:

An Industrial / Aeronautical engineer focus on Laboratory Test Means Design and Testing.

Required Educational Background and Experience:

4-5 years of experience in the field of benches design and testing, validation and verification. Ideally focus on A/C test benches.

Proven experience in data acquisition techniques.

Proven experience in sensors measurement and transducers.

Proven experience in CATIA V5 mechanical design.

Proven experience in LabVIEW/VERISTAND SW programming / mining and data exploitation.

Proven experience in interconnection wiring.

Ability to establish strategy, set direction and simplify or translate complex data/requirements to engage less specialist elements of the organisation. You will be able to communicate at all levels of the organisation.

Worked in close collaboration with national / trans-national stakeholders and multi-functional teams.

Ability to listen (customer needs-minded) and to challenge (improvement, Right First Time & efficiency focused).

Languages: Negotiation level of Spanish and English. German/French/Italian is considered a plus.

Vacation days plus additional days-off along the year.

Attractive salary.

Hybrid model of working when possible, promoting the work-life balance.

Collective transport service subject to working calendar/home location.

Benefits such as life insurance, health insurance, employee stock options, retirement plan, or study grants.

Possibility to collaborate in different social and corporate social responsibility initiatives.

Excellent upskilling opportunities and great development prospects in a multicultural environment.

Option to access to special rates in products & benefits.

Trigyn has a contractual opportunity for AI / ML Engineer. This resource will be working Remotely as per Central European Time zone (Valencia, Spain).

Background information:

AI / ML Engineers design, build, and operate machine and deep learning solutions that drive intelligent automation and data-driven decision-making across the organization. AI Engineers are responsible for deploying production-ready models, integrating them into scalable systems, and ensuring their reliability and performance through robust ML Ops practices. The incumbent will oversee the full machine learning lifecycle—including data preparation, model development, validation, deployment, monitoring, and retraining—using modern orchestration, CI/CD tools, and cloud platforms (e.g., Microsoft Azure). A key focus of this role includes advancing the organization’s capabilities in Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Generative AI, such as developing and fine-tuning Large Language Model-based chatbots, implementing text classification and extraction pipelines, and ensuring linguistic models align with business objectives and compliance requirements. The AI Engineer collaborates closely with data scientists, software engineers, and infrastructure teams to ensure AI solutions are secure, maintainable, and aligned with enterprise architecture and strategic goals.

Required Profile:

· +5 years’ experience as a researcher or engineer in AI, NLP and deep learning-related tasks. Having experience in an “industry” environment is a plus

· +5 years’ experience with text classification, information extraction, sequence labelling, model selection, evaluation, and result analysis.

· +3 years’ experience of multi-class and multi-task classification; LLMs; dialogue systems / chatbots, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), Agentic AI; adversarial architectures; online learning methodologies; model compression (pruning, quantization, distillation…); AI model serving for production settings.

· +3 years’ experience with Cloud platforms (e.g., Azure); API and web service development in python; web app development in Python; agile methodologies; containers and their orchestration; cloud computing solutions.

· Presence in the AI community, e.g., with experience in publishing research material.

This role encompasses all aspects of artificial intelligence R&D and ML Ops.

Daily activities include:

· Designing and implementing machine and deep learning-based AI & NLP workflows, algorithms and solutions in alignment with the business needs.

· Deliver scalable and maintainable solution using the latest cutting-edge technology.

· Identification and evaluation of strategic innovations (concepts, tools/languages, technologies, services) in the field of AI, NLP and deep learning.

· Constant personal development in new technologies.

Tasks

What you’ll be doing

Your work will directly shape how thousands of users experience JOIN. As a Senior Frontend Engineer, you’ll take strong ownership of delivering high-impact features end-to-end.

Own frontend feature delivery end-to-end – from technical discovery and solution design to implementation, testing, rollout, and monitoring in production.

Mentor less experienced engineers through collaboration and knowledge sharing (without formal people management responsibilities).

✔ Knowledge of API integration patterns and data fetching (REST, GraphQL).


✔ Familiarity with testing frameworks such as Jest, React Testing Library, or Cypress.

✔ Experience working collaboratively in cross-functional Agile teams.


✔ Strong focus on performance, accessibility, and usability.

Experience contributing to or maintaining a Design System.
Familiarity with Figma and UI collaboration workflows.
Exposure to backend concepts or full-stack environments.
Experience with analytics or experimentation tools (e.g. Statsig, A/B testing).


Benefits

Be Bold – Challenge the status quo and push boundaries.

We adapt quickly and embrace change in a growth-oriented startup environment
We continuously challenge ourselves and each other to improve

Titulo: Field Service Technician / EMS Commissioning Engineer (m/f/d)
Descripcion_Completa:
Job description
Are you a technician based in Spain with a passion for the energy transition? Do you enjoy combining focused remote work with hands-on project assignments in Germany?

We are looking for a technically versatile Field Service Technician / EMS Commissioning Engineer to join our team. You will be employed locally in Spain (via our payroll partner or as a freelance contractor) and travel to Germany approximately once per month for on-site installation and commissioning activities. During the remaining time, you will work remotely from your home office, focusing on software configuration and Energy Management System (EMS) programming.

The position is structured as follows:

One third: On-site installation and commissioning of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in Germany.
Two thirds: Remote programming and configuration of Energy Management Systems (EMS) from your home office in Spain.

Your Responsibilities

Hardware & Commissioning (33% — On-site in Germany)

Travel to project sites across Germany for approximately one week per month (flights and accommodation provided).
Perform on-site installation and commissioning of lithium-ion battery storage systems (up to 1500V DC).
Integrate and test Power Conversion Systems (PCS/inverters) with outputs of up to 800V AC.
Conduct functional testing of auxiliary systems, including HVAC units and liquid cooling systems.
Troubleshoot electrical and mechanical interfaces during system startup and commissioning.

Software & EMS Integration (66% — Remote from Spain)

Configure and program Energy Management Systems (EMS) remotely using manufacturer-specific software tools.
Support the remote integration of assets (batteries, inverters, meters) into the client’s local network infrastructure.
Set up and test communication protocols remotely (e.g., Modbus TCP/RTU, IEC 104, OPC UA).
Conduct remote commissioning sessions and provide technical support via phone and online tools to clients and local electricians.
Participate in virtual project meetings with German clients to understand project requirements and optimize system configurations.

Your Profile

Education: Vocational training (FP / Grado Superior) as an Electronics Technician, Automation Technician, or a degree in Electrical Engineering, Renewable Energy, or a related field.
Hardware Skills: Experience working with high-voltage components (up to 1,000V DC) and the ability to read electrical schematics. Experience with battery storage or inverter technology is required.
Software / IT Skills: Strong affinity for IT and networking. Experience configuring devices within local networks (IP addressing, subnetting, firewall basics) and working with configuration software. Knowledge of industrial communication protocols (Modbus, CAN bus, IEC 61850) is highly desirable.
Travel: Willingness to travel to Germany approximately one week per month (valid passport or EU ID required). A company vehicle will be provided during stays in Germany.
Spanish Language Skills: Native or fluent
German Language Skills: min. B2 (on-site communication / project meetings)
English Language Skils: Good technical reading skills

What We Offer

Remote Work: Full-time home office position based in Spain.
International Experience: Regular travel to Germany with all travel expenses covered (flights, accommodation, and rental/company car during on-site assignments).
Flexible Work Structure: No on-call duties. Intensive on-site project weeks balanced with focused remote work periods.
Modern Equipment: High-performance laptop, professional software licenses, and testing equipment provided.
Stability & Flexibility: Permanent employment under Spanish labor law (via our Employer of Record partner) or freelance cooperation, depending on your preference.

Interested in this opportunity? We look forward to hearing from you!

The Opportunity

At Univeris, a Software Development Tech Lead is a hands-on software engineer responsible for the technical design, development, and quality of their team’s technical deliverables. Tech Leads are collaborative by nature and lead by example, understanding that problem-solving and finding solutions are always more effective when done collectively. Tech Leads participate in roadmap and project planning, execute incremental plans with their team, play a role in communicating progress to stakeholders, and ultimately represent their product development team within the software development process.

The Role

The Software Development Tech Lead will be responsible for:

The quality of software delivered by the cross-functional product team, including adherence to development standards and frameworks.

Providing a high degree of visibility into the health and performance of the cross-functional team at all times.

The cross-functional team’s ability to meet delivery commitments.

The continuous improvement of the cross-functional team’s productivity and velocity.

Partnering with the Professional Services PM and Product Owner, working closely with them to effectively create, maintain, and refine (groom) the team's backlog:

Leading by example and providing leadership, mentorship, and inspiration to the product development team.

Partnering with the Product Owner, working closely with them to effectively create, maintain, and refine (groom) the team's backlog.

Acting as a role model for incorporating and applying Agile practices and methodologies.

Continuously collaborating with other Software Development Tech Leads, Software Development Managers, Architecture, and other technical teams involved in projects.

Leading the end-to-end technical vision of the project, overseeing the design and integration of complex systems, and identifying and mitigating technical risks early in the project lifecycle to minimize impact on delivery timelines and product quality.

Acting as an escalation point for technical decision-making, effectively evaluating and balancing benefits and trade-offs.

Understanding the need for development standards and best practices, contributing to them, and helping to enforce them.

Acting as a resource for their team regarding policies, procedures, and technical issues.

Performing design and code reviews of software components developed by other team members.

Reviewing current software to identify opportunities for improving performance and stability.

Promoting extensibility and reusability in application design and coding.


The Successful Candidate

Core Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science/related field or equivalent work experience.

8+ years of software development experience.

5+ years leading the design and development of high-performance transactional applications in Microsoft environments. Expert in server-side architectures and persistence frameworks, with a solid ability to integrate modern, scalable interfaces using Angular and web standards (HTML5/CSS3/JS).

2+ years of experience in a Team Lead role.

2+ years building cloud-native and API-centric applications.

Demonstrated experience and comfort in client-facing roles, with a focus on professional communication and relationship building.

Strong professional experience with Object-Oriented Programming (OOP).

Strong professional experience in iterative software delivery methodologies – Agile, SCRUM, SAFe, XP, etc.

Strong verbal, written, and presentation communication skills with the ability to communicate across all levels of users/management, including external clients and vendors.

Advanced English level (spoken and written), with experience in multicultural environments acting as the primary technical liaison between the development team, stakeholders, and end clients.

Demonstrated Qualifications (General)

Strong self-starter, leads by example, and collaborative.

Strong organizational and interpersonal skills with excellent attention to detail.

Ability to multitask, adapt to changing priorities, be decisive under ambiguity, and meet tight deadlines.

Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, results-oriented environment.

Proactivity in identifying areas of weakness and striving for improvement.

Aptitude and interest in quickly learning new and emerging technologies and methods.

Ability to assess risks and impacts on the existing system as new functionality is added.

Demonstrated Qualifications (Technical)

Building Cloud-Centric Applications: RESTful APIs, microservices, containers, Kubernetes, Terraform, Spring Boot.

Quality Technologies: Unit testing (xUnit, Fluent Assertions).

Working with SQL Server, stored procedures, stored functions, profiling, and database tuning, etc.

Titulo: Air Supply System Software Control Engineer
Descripcion_Completa:
Job Description:

Air Systems Control Engineer: Shape the Future of Flight

Are you an engineer with a passion for innovation and a knack for problem-solving? Do you want to play a key role in developing the next generation of greener, more efficient aircraft?

We're looking for an Air Systems Control Engineer to join our team at Airbus in Getafe (Madrid). You'll be at the heart of our Engineering department, contributing directly to the safety and comfort of millions of travelers.

What You’ll Do

As a core member of the Air Supply Systems team, you will:

Innovate and Optimize : Develop and integrate cutting-edge system architectures and equipment, ensuring our Environmental Control Systems (ECS) are reliable, highly efficient, and meet the highest safety standards. You'll be a vital part of our mission to explore green technologies for the Zero Emission challenges of tomorrow.

Take Control : Become the expert on the software and hardware that safely operates our Air Supply Systems. You will lead the charge in improving and adapting these systems, including groundbreaking work on projects like fiber optic sensing applications.

Solve Complex Challenges: Use your deep understanding of system behavior and control logic to support the daily operations of our air supply systems and resolve critical in-service issues.

Collaborate and Influence: Work directly with a dynamic network of international teams, from system suppliers and flight test teams to stakeholders across multiple organizations. Your expertise will be crucial in defining interfaces and driving project success.

Guide and Develop: You'll be part of a team that champions a new way of working, with a strong focus on professional development, well-being, and continuous improvement. Your contributions will be valued and celebrated.




Who We Are Looking For

We need a dedicated professional who is ready to make a significant impact on the future of aerospace. If you're a proactive problem-solver who thrives in a collaborative, forward-thinking environment, this is the role for you.

Join us at Airbus and help us build a more sustainable and comfortable future for air travel.

Ready to apply?

If you're excited by the chance to be at the forefront of aerospace engineering, we encourage you to submit your application and join our mission.

Required skills :

We are looking for candidates with the following skills and experience:

Master's degree / Diploma in Engineering or similar qualifications.

Work experience in System Engineering (V&V methodology).

Work experience in Software development and integration (mainly in IMA architecture).

Experience and knowledge on Airbus software & hardware development tools and processes:




SW/HW development reviews - FDR, FFR, ...

DO 178 & DO 254

SW/HW SOI 2/3/4



Experience in technical (international) coordination would be appreciated.

Experience in SW laboratory testing is considered a plus.

Good interpersonal and presentation skills (with business customers, internal and/or external, with Extended Enterprise subcontractors, etc.).

Good communication skills and ability to work within an international team.

Ability to abstract problems and issues in analytical way.

Ability to propose solutions and to challenge alternatives based on concrete business model in a constructive and respectful way.

Fluency in English is mandatory, Spanish or French or German is appreciated.

Team-oriented mindset with high communication skills, open mindset and willingness to learn complete your Profile.





You will become a member of a highly motivated and positively minded team.

WHICH BENEFITS WILL YOU HAVE AS AIRBUS EMPLOYEE?:

At Airbus we are focused on our employees and their welfare. Take a look at some of our social benefits:

Vacation days plus additional days-off along the year (Apróx. 35 days per year in total).

Attractive salary.

Hybrid model of working (40% per quarter) when possible, promoting the work-life balance.

Collective transport service subject to working calendar/home location.

Benefits such as life insurance, health insurance, employee stock options, retirement plan, or study grants.

On-site facilities (among others): free canteen, kindergarten, medical office.

Possibility to collaborate in different social and corporate social responsibility initiatives.

Excellent upskilling opportunities and great development prospects in a multicultural environment.

Option to access special rates in products & benefits.

You will utilize your knowledge of geospatial data and analytics while using tools such as Geographic Information Systems (GIS), Python, and visualization/analytical platforms (ex. Tableau, Alteryx). You will leverage a range of techniques such as geospatial clustering, network analysis, location-based optimization and predictive analytics/modeling. You will be expected to distill insights from your analysis and link them to business objectives. You will contribute to IP development, content development, and support internal marketing.


You will also have opportunities to experiment with emerging capabilities, including the selective use of GenAI to enhance analysis workflows and accelerate insight generation.


What You'll Bring

Education & Experience Qualifications:

1-3 years of relevant work experience in GeoAnalytics with advanced degree preferred
Relevant degree in geospatial related field such as: Geography, Geology, Environmental Science, Remote Sensing, Urban Planning, Statistics/GeoStatistics, Applied Mathematics, or Computer Science
Previous experience working in a global organization or professional services firm is preferred


Demonstrated experience in the following:

Conducting web-based research for open data sources; handling, understanding, and assessing new datasets; and stitching together disparate datasets to conduct custom analyses
Applying geospatial data, tools, and technology to solve problems for businesses across multiple topics
Technical experience in the following:
GIS software such as ESRI ArcGIS or QGIS
Data management skills (e.g. data modeling, QA/QC, querying, analysis)
Data science programming/coding in Python and/or R including use of spatial libraries/packages (eg GeoPandas, Rasterio, GDAL/OGR, Terra..)
Business Intelligence/data visualization platforms such as Tableau, Power BI, etc.
Applied descriptive statistics and analytical methods (e.g., correlation, regression, clustering) within machine learning frameworks to address geospatial problems

Web-GIS development skills (JavaScript, HTML, CSS) in ArcGIS environment (JS SDK, Python API, ExB custom widgets, etc.) are beneficial

Job Description:

Are you interested in joining into a team that offers you the possibility of having a multidisciplinary view of the A/C development process, having a close relationship will all the Centres of Competence (Design offices), Chief Engineering Offices, Services organization, System Suppliers, Airworthiness Authorities and in some cases with operational customers?

To support the Aircraft Development Safety team in Getafe, we are looking for a System Safety Engineer who will ensure that Airbus products are designed, developed and supported according to the relevant safety standards (associated to airworthiness regulations).

Tasks

As a member of the Aircraft Development Safety team, you will develop the following challenging tasks and activities:

Performing System Safety Assessment activities for military aircraft systems, supporting the development phases until certification.
Performing a systematic and comprehensive examination of System functions to identify and classify failure conditions of those functions according to their severity.
Establishing the System Safety requirements and performing preliminary evaluations to determine that the proposed architecture can reasonably be expected to meet the safety objectives.
Generating evidences to demonstrate in front of the Airworthiness Authorities that the final system architecture meets required System Safety requirements.
Performing analyses of Common-Cause (Particular Risk Analysis, Zonal Safety Analysis and Common Mode Analysis).
Performing Risk Analyses, according to the applicable guidance and standards, which are required to demonstrate the Continuous Airworthiness of the platform.
Manage and establish safety requirements at the interface to interacting systems.
Manage input from and output to interfacing technical domains and technology (processes, maintenance, human factos, aircraft performance, structures, quality, etc.).
Review, approval and application of supplier data regarding Safety and Reliability.

All these activities comprise all levels of development, from Aircraft level down to detailed requirements regarding hardware and Software.

Required skills

As a successful candidate, you will be able to demontrate the following skills and experience:

Master degree of Aeronautical or Industrial Engineer (or similar discipline).
Good Knowledge/understanding of Aircraft systems.
System Safety Assessment experience would be an advantage, at least System Engineering experience with interface to Safety Assessment/Airworthiness/Certification.
Advanced skills with MS Office are presumed.
Experience with Safety Tools, e.g. Isograph FaultTree+ will be valuable.
Good level of English Language.
Team working skills and problem-solving mind.

We’ve built the foundation, fueled the engine, and mapped the route. All that’s missing is the pilot to take the wheel for Tweakwise in France and Spain.

Founded in 2003, Orisha is a European software company supporting businesses in retail, real estate, healthcare, construction, and agri-food. With over €300M in revenue and 2,300 employees, the group provides vertical SaaS solutions that help companies manage and grow their operations.

Titulo: Senior Analyst - BCG Vantage, Tech and Digital Advantage
Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: Düsseldorf | Madrid

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


What You'll Do

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Topic Activation path within BCG's Tech and Digital Advantage (TDA) Practice Area, you will work in a growing global team, being involved in several of our fast-growing topics, such as (Gen)AI, Agentic AI, and digital transformation, helping shape our offering and working directly with case and proposal teams, providing expert advisory, analysis, and digital, tech, and GenAI insights. You’ll serve as a thought partner and content expert to case teams and topic/sector leaders, helping structure and solve complex issues.



You will support commercialization efforts for the topic/sector working, in conjunction with business leaders, through contributing analysis and insights to proposals, client workshops, AI demos, and marketing materials related to (Gen)AI and digital transformation. Additionally, as a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, you will contribute to developing intellectual property & assets for the business and assist in onboarding and training junior colleagues based on your topic/sector expertise.


YOU'RE GOOD AT

Solving client problems by developing and applying analytical and structured approaches in digital and AI contexts
Communicating confidently and credibly with case teams, stakeholders, and topic leaders
Working collaboratively in both in-person and virtual team settings
Navigating ambiguity and adapting to fast-paced, evolving digital topics
Bringing a curious and creative mindset, open to innovation and continuous improvement


What You'll Bring

2+ years of consulting experience in digital or AI-related transformation topics; candidates with consulting backgrounds strongly preferred
In lieu of consulting experience, 3+ years of relevant industry experience; 4–6+ years preferred
Strong understanding of AI/ML concepts, digital transformation narratives, agentic AI platforms, apps, ecosystems, market trends, use cases and enabling technologies / data / algorithms
Familiarity with software development projects, particularly those using Agile methodologies, is beneficial to understand the complexity of technological development and adoption.
Excellent Excel and PowerPoint skills; experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) is a plus
Programming experience (e.g., Python, SQL) not required but beneficial
Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred
Fluent English required; additional European languages are a plus
Strong business acumen, problem-solving, and communication skills


Who You'll Work With

As a Senior Analyst within the Digital & AI Transformation team, you will report to a Manager in the team and collaborate closely with case teams and topic leaders (Partners and MDPs). You’ll work with colleagues across BCG’s TDA network, combining cutting-edge AI, analytics, and digital expertise with strategic insights to deliver client and practice impact.


Additional info

BCG Vantage is a powerhouse of expertise, seamlessly embedded within Practice Areas and markets to drive extraordinary impact. It equips BCG case teams and clients with cutting-edge expertise, proprietary assets, actionable data, and transformative insights that empower our case teams and enable our clients to achieve their aspirations.

With global experts across 20 Practices, covering more than 100 topics, sectors and offers, plus four focused geographic markets, BCG Vantage delivers unparalleled depth and breadth of insight & expertise. BCG Vantage consists of three career paths, each playing a crucial role in delivering value to clients, case teams and practice teams.


Client Focus (Embedded Expertise)

Integrates into client-facing teams to deliver direct client impact through expertise, know-how, and proprietary tools and assets

Topic Activation (Sector Expertise with Proprietary Tool & Data Solutions)

Combines deep domain insights with our know-how and proprietary assets to address targeted challenges directly with clients and project teams; co-creates insights on industry trends and functional topics, proprietary data and tools, and teams to codify IP that all enables BCG to deliver excellent business value with its clients

Research (Practice Topic and Sector-Aligned Research and Insights)

Delivers actionable research and insights tailored to the client project’s needs; works with qualitative and quantitative data, conducts research and provides synthesized results from sources including commercial data, public and open source, company and industry research, macro-economic, commodities and trade data

Titulo: Senior IT Architect, Solutions - BCG Platinion
Descripcion_Completa:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


About BCG Platinion


BCG Platinion's presence spans across the globe, with offices in Asia, Europe, and South and North America. We achieve digital excellence for clients with sustained solutions to the most complex and time-sensitive challenge. We guide clients into the future to push the status quo, overcome tech limitations, and enable our clients to go further in their digital journeys than what has ever been possible in the past. At BCG Platinion, we deliver business value through the innovative use of technology at a rapid pace. We roll up our sleeves to transform business, revolutionize approaches, satisfy customers, and change the game through Architecture, Cybersecurity, Digital Transformation, Enterprise Application and Risk functions. We balance vision with a pragmatic path to change transforming strategies into leading-edge tech platforms, at scale.

Practice Area


BCG Platinion launched in Germany in 2000 to add deep technical expertise to the Boston Consulting Group’s existing capabilities. Today, our presence spans across the globe, with offices in Asia, Europe, and South and North America. Our New York-based North American team began in 2014 and in 2017 acquired MAYA Design, a Pittsburgh-based digital design and innovation lab, to grow our capabilities around technology and design.


We support our clients’ total digital transformation through technology, design, cybersecurity, and risk management & financial engineering capabilities. And together with BCG, BCG Platinion’s interdisciplinary team of technical experts enable customized technical solutions and accelerate delivery value through new business platforms, application consolidations, and major system implementations.


What You'll Do

As a Senior IT Architect, you will work closely with case team members to assess and coach our clients' IT teams, define IT strategies, architecture solutions and apply your technical know-how and understanding of business contexts. You will be responsible for owning the delivery for a specific project “module” and help develop technical concepts for that “module”. You will coach other project members and help lead other consultants in their own work stream when possible.




As part of our BCG Platinion consulting team, you would work on a variety of technology topics, applying your architectural consulting skills to strategic technology questions. You will be supported by an extensive training curriculum (the same programme as all BCG consultants), plus technology-specific training and mentoring to help you build your skills.


What You'll Bring


Experience:




Minimun 6-8 years of experience in IT architecture, solution design, and technical project management, with a proven record of delivering complex IT solutions and system integrations.
Spanish Mandatory
Fluent in English

Technical Skills:

Solution Architecture Design: Skilled in translating business needs into technical solutions, including requirements gathering, conceptual architecture, and detailed technical blueprints.
System Integration Expertise: Proficient in designing and implementing scalable, high-availability solutions integrating multiple systems and applications.
Technology Stack Proficiency: Hands-on experience with enterprise software platforms, middleware, APIs, and microservices architecture. Familiarity with cloud-based solutions (AWS, Azure, or GCP) is a plus.
Standards and Best Practices: Knowledge of IT governance frameworks, compliance standards (e.g., GDPR, DORA), and regulatory requirements to ensure secure and compliant systems.
Infrastructure Design: Experience in infrastructure automation, scalability planning, and disaster recovery strategies.




Desirable Expertise:

Enterprise Systems Knowledge: Exposure to ERP, CRM, and other enterprise-grade software systems across various industries such as Banking, Insurance, Energy, or Telecommunications.
Emerging Technologies: Understanding of DevOps practices, containerization (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes), and AI/ML-powered solutions for system enhancements.
Governance and Optimization: Ability to establish and enforce policies around resource optimization, system performance monitoring, and cost management.
Security Strategies: Familiarity with implementing security protocols and tools for continuous monitoring, vulnerability assessment, and threat detection.




Interpersonal & Leadership Skills:

Analytical Mindset: Capability to evaluate complex IT ecosystems and recommend optimization strategies.
Communication Excellence: Strong verbal and written skills to effectively liaise with business and technical stakeholders.
Leadership and Mentorship: Experience guiding development teams and collaborating across departments to ensure alignment with business objectives.




Industry Experience:

Proven experience working within industries such as Banking, Insurance, Energy, or Telecommunications, delivering tailored IT solutions that meet sector-specific requirements.


Boston Consulting Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, age, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity / expression, national origin, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected under national, provincial, or local law, where applicable, and those with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with applicable state and local laws.

What You'll Do

WHAT YOU'LL DO (AND WHY IT MATTERS)


As a Lead IT Architect, you will work closely with clients to understand their issues, define IT strategies, architecture solutions, win buy-in for your recommendations, and collaborate with fellow BCGers to transform client potential into performance. You will be given end-to-end responsibility for larger, highly technical and more complex 'modules' within a project, and begin to develop specialized knowledge to help you solve clients' problems. You will start to take on team lead responsibilities and demonstrate intellectual leadership


What You'll Bring

WHAT YOU'LL BRING (EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS)
Experience:
10+ years of experience in enterprise architecture, IT strategy development, and large-scale system design, with a demonstrated ability to align IT solutions with business objectives and lead cross-functional transformation initiatives.
Spanish Mandatory
Fluent in English


Technical Skills:

Enterprise Architecture Design: Expert in creating enterprise-wide IT architectures, including business, data, application, and technology layers, aligned with industry frameworks like TOGAF, Zachman, or SABSA.
Strategic Planning: Proficient in developing long-term IT roadmaps, aligning business and IT strategies, and driving digital transformation initiatives.
Technology Portfolio Management: Strong knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP), enterprise software systems, and middleware technologies, with a focus on interoperability and scalability.
Integration Mastery: Extensive experience in integrating legacy systems with modern solutions through APIs, microservices, and SOA frameworks.
Regulatory Compliance: In-depth understanding of compliance standards (e.g., GDPR, DORA, NIS2) and industry-specific regulations to ensure data security and privacy.
Disaster Recovery and Resilience: Skilled in designing robust disaster recovery and business continuity plans for enterprise systems.
Enterprise Security Strategies: Experience in implementing enterprise-level security architectures, including continuous monitoring, threat detection, and zero-trust models.


Desirable Expertise:

Governance and Risk Management: Proven ability to implement IT governance frameworks, optimize resource allocation, and manage risks associated with complex IT environments.
Emerging Technologies: Knowledge of advanced technologies like AI/ML, IoT, and blockchain, and their application in enterprise solutions.
Vendor Management: Expertise in evaluating and managing relationships with technology vendors and service providers to ensure delivery excellence.


Interpersonal & Leadership Skills:

Strategic Visionary: Ability to think strategically and design innovative IT solutions to drive business growth and competitive advantage.
Stakeholder Engagement: Excellent communication skills to influence C-level executives, present architectural strategies, and lead cross-functional teams.
Leadership and Mentorship: Proven leadership in guiding architects, developers, and IT teams while fostering a culture of innovation and collaboration.

Extensive experience in industries such as Banking, Insurance, Energy, Telecommunications, or Manufacturing, with a history of delivering enterprise-grade IT solutions tailored to sector-specific challenges.

The team Global Business Solutions within the IT@Components Shared Services acts as the central IT service provider for the Liebherr Group. Our task is to provide standardized, efficient and scalable IT services regarding global trade and compliance for the production and sales companies. Employees from several companies in Germany, France, Austria and Switzerland work in our IT@Components organization. We would now like to expand our global cooperation with employees in Spain.


That is why we are seeking a Service Manager Customs Solutions (m/f/d). The Service Manager Customs Solutions is responsible for the end‑to‑end provision, development, and continuous improvement of the IT service “Customs Solutions” for the Liebherr Group. The role combines service ownership, process expertise, project leadership, and technical coordination across global stakeholders.


The working location for this position will be in Madrid city where we are currently setting up a new office. We operate a hybrid model, requiring at least 40% of the working time on-site.

Creating passion: your responsibilities

Plan:

Align demand planning with the overall IT and Customs strategy to ensure service relevance and future readiness.
Assess impacts, risks, and dependencies of new demands across processes, systems, and organizational units.
Maintain and refine the long term service roadmap based on regulatory changes, technology evolution, and business priorities.

Build:

Document business requirements in a structured and comprehensive manner.
Collaborate with developers and the software vendor to assess feasible solution options.
Translate business requirements into actionable development tasks.
Coordinate integration activities with adjacent systems (ERP, logistics, trade compliance tool, etc.).
Validate delivery estimates, technical feasibility, and resource needs with development teams.

Deliver:

Coordinate change readiness activities, including communication, training, and stakeholder alignment.
Ensure that all changes are implemented with proper quality assurance.
Lead projects as the responsible project manager, ensuring adherence to timeline, budget, and quality standards.
Provide transparent reporting to all relevant stakeholders.

Run:

Ensure operational support for all participating companies.
Monitor service performance through KPIs and SLAs, ensuring continuous improvement.
Manage incident, problem, and change processes in alignment with ITSM standards (ITIL).
Work closely with the software vendor to continuously maintain and improve the solution.
Contributing your strengths: your qualifications
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Informatics, Industrial Engineering, or similar.
5+ years of professional hands-on experience in the specialist area of sales, shipping, customs or IT ERP consulting.
Experience in IT project management. Project management certification would be an advantage.
Knowledge of global trade processes, customs clearance and compliance would be an advantage.
Business fluent in English.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, and ability to adapt to an international working environment.
Willingness to travel international a few times a year, up to 10% of the time.
Our commitment to you: your benefits

At Liebherr, we believe people are at the heart of our success. As part of our international team, you’ll enjoy a secure role in a family-owned company that values innovation, collaboration, and long-term career growth:




Competitive compensation and benefits package that recognizes your expertise.
Flexible and hybrid working model.
Creative freedom and responsibility to shape processes and solutions in our global transformation.
Continuous learning and development with tailored training and certification opportunities.
Meal vouchers.
Life and accident insurance.
Option to include a premium private health insurance package as part of the flexible remuneration.
A safe, stable and international workplace within a trusted family business that invests in people.

Please only use the online application form on our website.


Please note that we do not accept applications via recruitment agencies for this position.

Have we awoken your interest? Then we look forward to receiving your online application. If you have any questions, please contact Karoliina Rissanen.

One Passion. Many Opportunities.

The company

Liebherr is a family-run technology company that is not only one of the largest construction machinery manufacturers in the world, but also offers high-quality, user-oriented products and services in many other areas. The Group employs nearly 50,000 people in more than 140 companies on all continents.

Location

Liebherr IT Shared Service Centre Ibérica, S.L.

Parque Norte. Alamo building Serrano Galvache, 56

28033 Madrid

Spain (ES)

Karoliina Rissanen

Project Manager - Museografía y Espacios Expositivos (H/M/D)

Solo se valorarán candidaturas que adjunten carta de motivación para el puesto

Sobre la posición

En PROASUR buscamos incorporar a una persona con visión estratégica para liderar proyectos internacionales singulares. Si te motiva gestionar el ciclo de vida completo de un proyecto, desde el detalle técnico hasta el éxito financiero, y buscas un entorno donde ejercer con autonomía y responsabilidad, esta es tu oportunidad.

Tus Responsabilidades Principales

1. Gestión y Salud Financiera del Proyecto

Liderazgo Económico: Serás responsable de la cuenta de resultados de tus proyectos. Tu objetivo será optimizar los recursos para maximizar el margen, asegurando siempre la calidad de ejecución y la satisfacción del cliente.
Reporte y Análisis: Elaboración de informes mensuales de seguimiento (certificaciones, cashflow y control de costes), identificando proactivamente desviaciones y proponiendo soluciones para asegurar el resultado final.

2. Gestión Integral del Alcance (Scope Management)

Visión 360º: Gestión global del alcance contractual, asegurando la correcta integración de todas las disciplinas (AV, iluminación, estructuras, acabados, contenidos, etc.).
Control de Cambios: Detectarás y gestionarás las variaciones y solicitudes del cliente, elaborando los presupuestos de variaciones necesarios en coordinación con la Oficina Técnica y proveedores, protegiendo los intereses del proyecto.

3. Planificación y Estrategia Técnica

Planificación Maestra: Definición de la hoja de ruta del proyecto. Establecerás hitos (milestones) claros y realistas, asegurando que todo el equipo (interno y externo) esté alineado con los plazos y objetivos críticos.
Profundidad Técnica: Análisis de la documentación contractual para elaborar especificaciones de compra precisas y definir la estrategia de ejecución (EDT) en herramientas como Asana.

4. Coordinación de Operaciones: Compras, Taller y Logística

Activación de la Cadena de Suministro: Liderarás la planificación de compras y subcontrataciones, resolviendo dudas técnicas y asegurando que los materiales lleguen en tiempo y forma.
Enlace con Producción: Mantendrás una comunicación fluida con Taller y Producción, supervisando la calidad y el avance mediante las Órdenes de Trabajo, y reportando el estado en las reuniones de seguimiento operativo.
Logística Eficiente: Coordinación con el departamento logístico para planificar envíos complejos y recepciones en obra, anticipando las necesidades de medios auxiliares y montaje.

5. Gestión Documental y Calidad (QA/QC)

Rigor Administrativo: Supervisión del flujo documental del proyecto (planos, fichas técnicas, entregables), garantizando que todo envío al cliente cumpla con los estándares de calidad de PROASUR.
Gestión de Incidencias: Liderarás la resolución de No Conformidades (NCs) e incidencias, tanto en fase de ejecución como en garantía, coordinando a los equipos de Calidad y proveedores para un cierre eficaz.

6. Relación con el Cliente y Cierre

Interlocución Clave: Serás el principal punto de contacto con el cliente tras la adjudicación, construyendo una relación de confianza y transparencia.
Gestión de Cobros: Supervisión de los hitos de facturación y preparación de la documentación soporte (albaranes, certificaciones) para asegurar el flujo de caja del proyecto.
Cierre Exitoso: Responsabilidad sobre el cierre administrativo y económico, obtención de certificados de buena ejecución y análisis de lecciones aprendidas para la mejora continua del equipo.

Perfil y Requisitos del Candidato

Para asegurar el éxito en esta posición, buscamos un perfil técnico con visión de negocio, capaz de desenvolverse en entornos internacionales complejos.

Formación y Experiencia:

Titulación: Arquitectura, Ingeniería (Industrial/Técnica) o formación superior equivalente con enfoque en gestión de proyectos.
Trayectoria: Entre 5 y 8 años de experiencia demostrable gestionando proyectos completos (ciclo de vida 360º), valorándose especialmente la experiencia en museografía, retail (fit-out), construcción singular o ferias internacionales.

Idiomas:

Inglés: Nivel Negociación (C1) imprescindible. Capacidad para defender propuestas, discutir contratos y gestionar reuniones técnicas fluidas.

Competencias Técnicas y Gestión (Hard Skills):

Control Económico y Analítico: Alta capacidad para el análisis de costes y gestión de proveedores. Buscamos a alguien que domine el dato financiero del proyecto.
Herramientas: Conocimiento de software de diseño (lectura de planos, CAD, visualización 3D) y dominio de herramientas de planificación y gestión de tareas (Project, Asana o similares).
Calidad: Rigor en el cumplimiento de los estándares corporativos y normativas técnicas.

Habilidades de Liderazgo y Personales (Soft Skills):

Autonomía Radical y Responsabilidad: Buscamos profesionales con capacidad de decisión ("Ownership"), que gestionen con criterio propio y eleven soluciones estratégicas, no solo reportes de estado.
Gestión de Equipos y Comunicación: Habilidad para liderar equipos multidisciplinares y coordinar proveedores externos, manteniendo la motivación y el orden en momentos de alta demanda.
Movilidad: Disponibilidad para viajar internacionalmente para supervisiones críticas, arranques o cierres de proyecto.

Compromiso con la Igualdad

Titulo: Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws specialist
Descripcion_Completa:
Job Description:

Join the Flight Physics Centre of Competence (CoC) at Airbus Defence & Space (AD&S) Engineering, a dynamic and inclusive German-Spanish organization dedicated to shaping the future of air power solutions. We are seeking a passionate and innovative Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws Engineer to join our team and help us redefine the skies. At Airbus, we believe in the power of diversity and are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive.

The selected candidate will join the Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws Team in Getafe to contribute to the development of the on-going projects (EF2000, FCAS, MRTT, SIRTAP, M&L;...)

Flight the Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws Domain mission is to provide our customers safely flying air vehicles with the best possible performance, agility and handling/flying qualities by designing extraordinary control laws and performing comprehensive clearance assessments within the full flying envelope. Main task and responsibilities of Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws Domain are:

Definition of the flight control system requirements (aircraft stability requirements, aircraft control power requirement, sensors requirements, actuators requirements, electric and hydraulic flight control system requirements,...)
Design of the flight control laws algorithms thar provide system stability augmentation, command system, autopilots and automatic function to reduce pilot workload and ensure optimal Handling Qualities
Produce a comprehensive flight clearance that ensure a safe flight test envelope expansion, data gathering of required test data and demonstration of the desired performances of the aircraft flight control system
Produce aircraft simulation models, simulation environment build-up to allow the development, validation and verification of the flight control laws software design as well as flight test matching of the aircraft dynamic models for training simulators accreditation
Produce the documentation dossiers to grant product Handling Qualities certification and qualification
Contribute to flight control system development and certification that is a key complex system in all AIRBUS products that significantly contribute to our customer satisfaction

What you will do

Shape the Future: Contribute to current and future Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws projects, R&D, certification processes, and negotiations with certification authorities. Your insights will drive innovation and excellence.
Innovate and Inspire: Support the design phase of new programs and derivatives of existing products, evaluating the impact of configuration changes on aircraft handling qualities and mission effectiveness. Your creativity will be the catalyst for ground breaking solutions.
Customer-Centric Approach: Conduct handling qualities studies and flight control laws enhancements to support sales campaigns, RFPs, and customer inquiries. Provide operational tools and documentation for flight operations, and assist with flight data analysis for development, certification, training, and incident investigations. Your customer-focused mindset will ensure we deliver exceptional service.
Strategic Implementation: Develop and implement roadmaps for tool harmonization, data management, and marketing of simulation services. Drive the Flight Physics strategy and grow our service portfolio. Your strategic thinking will help us stay ahead of the curve.
Quality and Excellence: Ensure the highest standards in technical dossier content and presentation. Implement digitalization, capability, and technology roadmaps while ensuring R&T, R&D, and program delivery. Your attention to detail will set the bar for excellence.
Collaborate and Lead: Contribute to team and program activities, fostering synergies and continuous improvement. Communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders, demonstrating strong problem-solving and organizational skills. Your leadership will inspire and motivate the team.
Customer Focus: Inspire confidence and credibility with internal and external customers, anticipating their needs and delivering exceptional service. Your customer-centric approach will build lasting relationships.

RESPONSIBILITIES/MAIN TASKS

The jobholder will be responsible of the following task:

Perform the stability analysis of aircraft relevant configuration in failure free and under degraded system states
Analyze nonlinear aircraft simulation in failure free and under degraded system states to ensure adequate aircraft response, departure resistance and adequate handling qualities’
Maintain and upgrade simulation models, pre and post processing tools to perform above mentioned tasks
Perform trade-off to identify root cause of the deficiencies found during the flight clearance activities
Organize flight dynamic manned simulation activities required to answer programs needs during a flight clearance loop
Define the flight test campaign required for simulation models data gathering and for Handling Qualities assessment
Propose flight control laws updates based on clearance and flight test results
Flight test analysis to update/validate simulation models and for the preparation of handling qualities certification/qualification dossier

SKILLS

We are looking for candidates with the following skills and experience:

Educated in an Aeronautical Engineering degree (MASTER in air vehicles)
Proficiency in English at a negotiation level. German knowledge will be valued
Knowledge of FORTRAN, C, Matlab, Simulink
Experience in flight dynamics activities will be consider as a plus. Your expertise will be invaluable to our team.
Passion for Improvement: A commitment to continuous improvement and a willingness to roll up your sleeves to get the job done. Your dedication will drive our success.
Strategic Thinking: The ability to think strategically and execute methodically. Your vision will guide our projects to new heights.
Resilience: The capability to work under pressure and travel as required. Your adaptability will be key to our success.
Integrity: A strong awareness of compliance risks and a commitment to acting with integrity. Your ethical standards will uphold our reputation.
Capability to integrate in a multi-cultural team
High adaptability to work on new topics, new business and in a network mode *competences

At Airbus Defence & Space, we are committed to fostering an inclusive culture where everyone can reach their full potential. We encourage women to apply and be part of a team that is transforming the future of aviation. Together, we can achieve greatness and make a lasting impact on the world.

We shape your career in a dynamic and supportive environment. We offer opportunities for professional development and advancement.
Contribute to ground breaking projects that redefine air power solutions. Your work will have a lasting impact on the world.
Enjoy a balanced work environment that supports your personal and professional growth. We understand the importance of a healthy work-life balance.
Join a community of like-minded professionals who support and uplift each other. Your voice will be heard, and your ideas will be valued.

At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible, we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking

This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.

Job Description:

An opportunity for MRTT Industrial Quality Support has arisen within Airbus Operations in Getafe (Madrid).

You will ensure that the company’s operational processes (develop, fulfil, support and source) comply with the quality assurance requirements, standards and internal or third party certification/approvals as defined in the Quality Management System (QMS) and quality plan for hardware/software/services.

You will devise and validate the hardware/software/services' control and inspection plan and ensure its proper implementation (using traditional and new methods, e.g. virtual/augmented reality).

You will monitor the conformity & attestation status.

Where deviations occur, you will be involved in the handling of the non- conformance including analysis of the deviations, monitoring of the corrective and preventive actions, as well as contributing to the improvement of the quality standards.

As such, you will collect, analyze, visualize and interpret the relevant process and/or in service performance data using available means (manual and automatic) to identify early process variations and to take effective actions by means of root cause analysis to mitigate and resolve any quality issues by supporting a robust PPS method application.

You will provide the evidence of conformity to processes and requirements to Programme Quality Assurance to ensure the satisfaction of customer requirements.

In new product / process / service development you will ensure that operational processes' lessons learnt and risk analysis are done early, and that relevant preventive quality standards are used in order to mitigate the risks and that a rigorous Development Plan (multi- functional reviews, maturity gates, ...) is followed.

You will implement & operate Prevention Means (such as APQP, DFMEA) . In case of software development you will ensure software and data integrity, verify and validate/certify the software and data in compliance with the appropriate standard for its specific application, e.g. on-board software, ground test software for prototype, small series production, simulation models, decision making support systems.

If applicable (within Procurement Quality), you will carry out supplier development and surveillance, supporting the qualification of supplier manufacturing processes and setting applicable requirements to suppliers.

If authorized, you will support the company's certifications and interfaces with external quality authorities (ISO, IAQG, EASA, etc.,).

KEY DELIVERABLES: Control and Inspection Plan, Product/Service Conformity for a given domain, Non Conformance Analysis and Action Plan, Supplier and Development Assurance Plan, APQP Key Business Deliverables (when owner).

KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES: to demonstrate conformity of processes and requirements for a given domain to Programme Quality Assurance and External Authorities (when authorized), to drive non conformity management at domain level for Programme Quality Assurance.

Resumen del Trabajo:

¿Qué es House of Candy?

House of Candy es una experiencia inmersiva única que celebra la alegría, la dulzura y el placer sin culpa.

Dentro de esta experiencia, la Concept Store es la entrada perfecta: un espacio visualmente llamativo donde se venden productos de regalo, se sirve helado artesanal y café de especialidad, y se ofrece una primera pincelada del universo divertido y colorido de HoC.

La persona en este rol es clave para crear una primera impresión excepcional. Tu responsabilidad será que todo fluya con energía, orden y actitud positiva.

No es necesario tener experiencia previa en café de especialidad o helado (aunque se valorará), pero sí es imprescindible tener muchas ganas de aprender, cuidar el detalle y trabajar en equipo.

Además, en ciertos momentos, también ejercerás funciones de Controlador/a de salas y atención en la experiencia, por lo que se requiere versatilidad, compromiso y una actitud activa y resolutiva.

Responsabilidades Clave:

Atención al Visitante

Recibir a los visitantes con entusiasmo y ayudarles con cualquier consulta u orientación sobre House of Candy.
Asegurar que el ambiente sea acogedor y alegre desde la entrada.
Informar sobre la experiencia y los tipos de entrada.

Servicio de Café de Especialidad

Preparar los distintos cafés de especialidad del menú con atención al detalle.
Participar en las formaciones impartidas por nuestro partner Onyva.
Mantener la limpieza y organización de la tienda.

Servicio de Helado Artesanal

Servir helado de manera eficiente y atractiva.
Participar en las formaciones impartidas por nuestro partner Parallelo Gelato.
Contribuir a que el servicio de producto sea parte de la experiencia HoC.

Venta y Gestión de Tienda

Cobrar productos con precisión utilizando el software de punto de venta.
Informar sobre los artículos de regalo de la tienda y ayudar con recomendaciones.
Controlar el stock, reponer producto y asegurar una presentación impecable.
Realizar el cierre de caja y tareas básicas de administración diaria.

Apoyo a la Experiencia (Funciones de Controlador/a):

Supervisar el acceso a la experiencia cuando sea necesario, controlando entradas y gestionando el flujo de visitantes.
Vigilar las salas e instalaciones para asegurar su buen uso y detectar cualquier incidencia.
Resolver dudas y dar soporte a los visitantes durante el recorrido.
Garantizar el cumplimiento de las normas de House of Candy y actuar como referente amable, atento y resolutivo.
Ofrecer asistencia puntual en eventos o actividades especiales.

Se requiere:

Ser buena persona.
Ganas de trabajar y aprender.
Nivel medio/alto de inglés.
Espíritu de equipo.
Actitud proactiva y sentido de la responsabilidad.
Flexibilidad para asumir distintas funciones según las necesidades del día.
Ganas de involucrarse en una experiencia distinta y hacerla especial desde el primer contacto.

En IT8 ofrecemos un servicio de primera clase para hacer realidad proyectos de ingeniería. Nuestros más de 19 años de experiencia en los sectores de la automoción y la logística nos convierten en un socio excelente para desarrollar y ejecutar proyectos técnicos estratégicos en todo el mundo.

Nuestro excepcional equipo de expertos, con sede en Estados Unidos, México, España, Alemania y la India, ofrece un servicio profesional y soluciones combinadas con altos estándares de calidad.

Somos una empresa de ingeniería líder que busca un/una Electrical Installation Leader con talento y experiencia para unirse a nuestro equipo para distintos proyectos.

Como Electrical Installation Leader, se encargará de la implementación mecánica y eléctrica satisfactoria de los sistemas de producción automatizados mediante la coordinación y supervisión eficaces de las actividades de instalación.

Principales tareas

Montaje eléctrico in situ. Responsable del montaje in situ de paneles eléctricos, cajas de distribución de campo, interfaces hombre-máquina (HMI), sistemas de comunicación y conductos eléctricos, garantizando su correcta instalación y funcionalidad.
Cableado y tendido de redes. Supervisar y ejecutar la instalación y organización del cableado de redes, garantizando una comunicación eficiente entre los dispositivos y el cumplimiento de las especificaciones técnicas del proyecto.
Conexión de mangueras y componentes eléctricos. Gestionar la conexión adecuada de mangueras eléctricas y cableado a armarios eléctricos, dispositivos de campo y elementos operativos dentro de la línea de producción.
Conexión a equipos robóticos y de producción. Garantizar las conexiones eléctricas correctas a robots, herramientas, estaciones de herramientas, manipuladores y sistemas automatizados, cumpliendo con los requisitos operativos y las normas de seguridad.
Identificación y etiquetado de cables y mangueras. Supervisar y ejecutar el etiquetado correcto de cables y mangueras para mantener una organización clara y facilitar las futuras actividades de mantenimiento y resolución de problemas.
Interpretación de diagramas eléctricos. Leer e interpretar diagramas y esquemas eléctricos complejos para guiar las instalaciones, detectar problemas y garantizar el cumplimiento de la documentación de diseño.
Tendido e instalación de cables de alimentación y control. Realizar el tendido, la fijación y la conexión de cables de alimentación (alta y baja tensión), cables de control y cableado de señales, garantizando el funcionamiento fiable de todos los sistemas eléctricos.
Informes a la dirección y a los clientes. Mantener una comunicación clara y coherente con los supervisores directos y los clientes, proporcionando información actualizada sobre el progreso de la instalación, las cuestiones técnicas y el estado de finalización.
Participación en la formación interna. Participar en programas de formación interna para mantenerse al día en cuanto a habilidades técnicas, procedimientos de seguridad y protocolos de la empresa, promoviendo el desarrollo profesional continuo.
Garantizar que los procesos generen los resultados esperados y cumplan los requisitos del sistema SGI.

Formación en eléctrica o similar.

Excelentes habilidades organizativas y de gestión del tiempo.
Fuertes habilidades de liderazgo y gestión de equipos.
Orientación al cliente y capacidad para resolver problemas.
Excelentes habilidades comunicativas e interpersonales.

Sobre Yommymascota

En Yommymascota conectamos de forma auténtica con nuestra comunidad de "pet lovers". Somos una marca dinámica en plena expansión y buscamos profesionales que no solo dominen la cámara, sino que sientan verdadera pasión por el mundo animal.

Misión del Puesto

Buscamos a la voz y el rostro oficial de nuestras plataformas digitales. Tu objetivo será liderar nuestras sesiones de venta en vivo y generar contenido audiovisual de alto impacto desde nuestro set profesional. Buscamos a alguien con la energía necesaria para captar la atención de la audiencia y la capacidad técnica para entregar piezas finales listas para su publicación.

Responsabilidades Principales

Host de Live Shopping: Conducción y dinamización de sesiones de venta en directo, interactuando con la comunidad y presentando productos de forma persuasiva.
Producción de Contenido: Participación activa en rodajes presenciales para campañas de marketing, tutoriales y lanzamientos.
Edición y Postproducción: Edición completa de los vídeos grabados, optimizando el formato para TikTok, Instagram Reels y YouTube.
Colaboración Creativa: Aportar ideas frescas y guiones basados en las tendencias actuales del mercado de mascotas.

Requisitos Esenciales

Carisma y Comunicación: Excelente presencia frente a cámara, dicción impecable y capacidad de improvisación.
Dominio de Edición: Experiencia avanzada en software de edición (CapCut, Adobe Premiere, etc.).
Presencialidad Obligatoria: Capacidad para desplazarse a nuestro estudio/oficina para todas las sesiones de grabación y edición. (Este puesto no admite modalidad de trabajo en remoto).
Perfil Comercial: Capacidad para integrar técnicas de venta de forma orgánica en el discurso.

Condiciones de la Oferta

Flexibilidad de Jornada: Ofrecemos opciones tanto de Jornada Completa como de Media Jornada, según el perfil y la disponibilidad del candidato/a.
Entorno Profesional: Trabajo en un set de grabación equipado con todo el material necesario para una producción de primer nivel.
Salario: Competitivo, acorde a la experiencia y la modalidad de jornada elegida.

Aufgaben

You will take ownership of our Google Cloud Platform environments and ensure smooth integration between internal and partner data centers.

You will build and maintain Kubernetes clusters as the foundation of our development and production environments.

You will set up and continuously improve the core platform infrastructure, keeping developer experience and scalability in mind.

You will ensure a secure and reliable cloud environment, including access management, identity control (e.g. Keycloak), and network protection.

You will support development teams in deploying, scaling, and monitoring their applications in the cloud – from setup to incident response.

You will enable communication between services across cloud and on-premise systems.

You will develop and optimise our Infrastructure as Code setup with Crossplane, Terraform or OpenTofu to automate and standardise deployments.

You will contribute to initiatives around monitoring, observability, and secret management (e.g. Google Secret Manager, Cloudflare, ArgoCD).

You will collaborate closely with teams across Development, Security, and Architecture to drive our overall cloud and platform strategy forward.

Qualifikationen

5+ years of experience in Cloud engineering or DevOps.

Proven hands-on experience with Google Cloud, AWS, or Azure.

Strong knowledge of Kubernetes, Terraform/OpenTofu, and modern CI/CD pipelines (e.g. ArgoCD, GitLab CI, or CircleCI).

Experience with Linux, shell scripting and APIs/networking fundamentals.

Understanding security, monitoring, and Identity & Access management concepts.

Ideally, a development background – you think like a developer and build with developer experience in mind.

Collaborative mindset with great communication skills – you enjoy partnering with cross-functional teams.

Fluent in English (German is a plus).

Descripcion_Completa:
SOBRE NOSOTROS:

​En Piles & Co (Valencia), nos especializamos en proyectos de construcción industrial, reformas complejas y diseño de alto nivel. Buscamos elevar nuestra capacidad técnica interna incorporando a un Ingeniero/a Industrial que no solo dirija la ejecución, sino que tenga la capacidad de calcular, dimensionar y validar técnicamente cada decisión del proyecto.

​TU RETO:

​Te incorporarás para cubrir una baja de maternidad en la Jefatura de Obra, pero con una misión estratégica: internalizar la ingeniería.

Queremos que uses tu capacidad de cálculo (CYPE) y modelado (BIM/Escáner 3D) para optimizar las obras, detectar errores de proyecto antes de construir y ahorrar costes mediante soluciones técnicas eficientes.

​TUS RESPONSABILIDADES:

​1. Jefatura de Obra (Gestión):

​Planificación y dirección técnica de obras industriales y residenciales.
​Control económico (costes, certificaciones) y coordinación de industriales.

​2. Ingeniería y Cálculo (CYPE - Tu Valor Diferencial):

​Cálculo de Estructuras: Comprobación y redimensionado de elementos estructurales (metálicos y hormigón) con CYPECAD / CYPE 3D.
​Cálculo de Instalaciones: Dimensionado y justificación de instalaciones (Electricidad, Climatización, Fontanería) cumpliendo el CTE, utilizando la suite de CYPE (CYPELEC, CYPETERM, etc.).
​Legalización de instalaciones industriales.

​3. Digitalización (BIM y Escáner 3D):

​Levantamiento de estados actuales mediante Escáner 3D / Nube de Puntos.
​Integración de los cálculos y mediciones en el flujo de trabajo BIM (Revit) para una ejecución "cero errores".

​LO QUE BUSCAMOS (REQUISITOS):

​Formación: Ingeniería Industrial (Superior o Técnica) o Ingeniería Civil.
​Experiencia: Mínimo 3-4 años en puesto similar (Oficina Técnica + Obra).
​Dominio de Software (Imprescindible):
​Paquete CYPE (Cálculo de estructuras e instalaciones).
​Metodología BIM (Revit, Navisworks).
​Gestión de Nubes de Puntos (Escaneado 3D).
​Perfil: Buscamos un ingeniero "completo". Alguien capaz de calcular la viga en la oficina por la mañana y supervisar su montaje en la obra por la tarde.

​LO QUE OFRECEMOS:

​Contrato: Sustitución de maternidad (aprox. 8 meses) con altísima posibilidad de continuidad. Si demuestras que tu capacidad de cálculo nos ahorra dinero y problemas, el puesto se convertirá en indefinido como Responsable Técnico.
​Salario: Banda salarial competitiva de 32.000 € a 40.000 € Bruto/Año (según el nivel de autonomía en el cálculo con CYPE).
